title: (BUG) desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs: Mutex::lock().unwrap() panics on poisoned mutex
description: Lines 75 and 114 of desktop/src-tauri/src/lib.rs call state.lock().unwrap(). If any previous Tauri command handler panicked while holding the mutex, Rust marks the mutex as poisoned; all subsequent lock() calls return Err(PoisonError), and .unwrap() panics. This crashes the entire Tauri process (the desktop application). Fix: use .lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) to recover from poison, or restructure to avoid holding the mutex across any panic-capable code paths.
